artifacts.title: How Petvisor consolidated five payroll systems into one with Deel | Deel
artifacts.normalized_text: How Petvisor consolidated five payroll systems into one with Deel 5 Payroll systems consolidated into one 3 New countries entered 1 Global payroll manager Industry Software Region North America Company Size 51-200 The Problem Bringing multiple companies-and systems-under one roof Petvisor is a technology company in the pet care industry, specializing in solutions that support veterinary practices and other pet care providers. After acquiring several companies, the Petvisor team struggled with fragmented global payroll and hiring systems that lacked centralized processes and visibility, posing significant compliance and operational risks. "Our HR and payroll systems were disjointed and unmanageable," said Sophie Karagianes, Chief People Officer at Petvisor. "We needed Deel's centralized solution to scale." The Solution Gaining full oversight with Deel Payroll Payroll was one of the first operations to improve. "We initially evaluated other providers, but the costs exceeded payroll itself," said Karagianes. "We chose Deel because its suite of solutions uniquely fits our broad needs". Deel Payroll consolidated 5+ HR and payroll functions into one platform, allowing a single person to manage all global payrolls. artifacts.normalized_text: How MarqVision expanded its global presence compliantly with Deel 30+ global workers hired 8+ markets hiring Industry Artifical Intelligence, Intellectual Property Protection Region North America Company Size 51-200 The Challenge Complex local regulations hindered global expansion Founded in 2019, MarqVision is a US-based trailblazing startup providing integrated AI-powered IP protection solutions. It has received approximately $41M of cumulative investments, with notable investors including SoftBank Ventures, Y Combinator, and Altos Ventures. MarqVision kicked off its global expansion in 2022, starting with Europe. But scaling internationally came with challenges-complex hiring laws, cross-border payments, and compliance risks. As the company expanded its global footprint, it needed a fast, seamless, and compliant way to onboard over 30 international workers across eight countries: Belgium, France, Italy, Portugal, Spain, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, and China. "Choosing Deel was easy-they offer unmatched compliance and HR support that scales with fast-growing companies like ours. They've been the best partner for executing our global hiring strategy." - Yunjung (Rina) Bae, Director of People, MarqVision The Solution Improving operational efficiency and compliance By adopting EOR and Deel Payroll, MarqVision successfully streamlined its global expansion and talent management processes. artifacts.title: How Deel helped Clara expand its business without increasing expenses | Deel
artifacts.normalized_text: How Clara expand its business without increasing expenses with Deel 5 Days saved per hire 9+ Countries in which they hired $215K+ Saved per year on local payroll costs The Company Clara helps companies manage expenses like never before Clara simplifies managing business expenses with an integral solution composed of corporate credit cards, a payment product, and a digital expense control platform. Carolina Astaiza, Global People Director of Clara, said, "Doing business is a challenge, especially from the administration. You invest time and resources in reviewing, correcting, and managing expenses and invoicing. Here Clara is born, offering an integral solution so that the companies have an easier way to order and control the finances, freeing time to focus on the core of the business." The Problem Offering the team the possibility of working from where they are Clara was founded during the pandemic in a virtual and remote environment. From the beginning, they knew that talent isn't tied to location, so they needed a solution to offer that same flexibility without so many complications. As they grew from their home country of Mexico to Brazil and Colombia, taking advantage of technology and the remote work boom became more urgent and tangible. Astaiza explained that the team wanted to hire local and global talent that was remote to "show them that we value their space, time, and flexibility." Having talent nearby was no longer as important as landing the best talent they could

Without a unified system providing a single view of labor costs, the organization risked inefficiencies in navigating economic shifts and ensuring multi-market compliance. The Estonia E-Residency Programme, launched in 2014, empowers entrepreneurs worldwide to start EU-based companies while managing their business from anywhere, entirely online. Estonia is the first country to offer e-Residency, a government-issued digital identity that gives access to Estonia's transparent business environment. artifacts.normalized_text: Business.Scoop » Deel Unveils Akai By Deel, An AI-Powered Platform To Automate Operations Workflows     Business Scoop Network ### Deel Unveils Akai By Deel, An AI-Powered Platform To Automate Operations Workflows May 12, 2026 PressRelease Press Release - Deel Akai is designed to work for any ops team that runs repetitive, high-volume workflows across any system. Created as Deel's own solution, Akai runs 100% of Deel's operations and is now made available for ops teams everywhere. Auckland, NZ, 12 May, 2026 - Deel, today launched Akai By Deel, an agent platform built to automate operational workflows. Unlike static automation tools, Akai runs by using AI agents that can learn, execute, and improve workflows for repetitive tasks. Ops teams spend hours to process payments across multiple portals, file compliance submissions, reconcile FX reports, and keep data in sync across systems that don't talk to each other. At scale, it's relentless - and most of it still happens manually. What ops teams actually need is automation they can build themselves, that runs on any system, learns from every run, and keeps humans in control. Deel faced the same challenge while managing payroll, payments, and compliance operations for millions of workers globally. Existing tools could not support the scale, complexity, and compliance requirements involved, leading Deel to build Akai internally. Today, thousands of workflows are already running across 100% of Deel's operations teams - Finance,

artifacts.title: Deel chooses Stripe to create a stablecoin wallet to help millions of ...
artifacts.normalized_text: Deel chooses Stripe to create a stablecoin wallet to help millions of contractors hold, earn, and spend globally SAN FRANCISCO AND DUBLIN-Stripe, the programmable financial services company, today announced that Deel is using Stripe to enable millions of contractors across 150+ countries to hold, earn, and spend a US dollar backed balance. Deel is a global payroll and compliance platform that supports more than 40,000 businesses and 1.5 million workers worldwide. Today, the company is launching a stablecoin wallet to help their contractors around the world hold earnings in DLUSD, Deel's USD-denominated balance, be eligible to earn rewards on their holding, and spend anywhere. In markets like Argentina and Turkey, a salary paid in local currency can fluctuate between 20% and 40% of its value in a single year. According to Deel, in 2025, 85% of contractors in Argentina wanted to be paid in US dollars rather than Argentine pesos. That's now possible with Stripe's stablecoin infrastructure. "As Deel simplifies global work for more businesses and workers around the world, the way people get paid has to evolve with it," said Alex Bouaziz, Co-founder and CEO of Deel. "Contractors want dollar-backed pay they can hold, earn on, and spend without leaving the platform. Stripe's stablecoin stack gives us the infrastructure to make that happen, simply and compliantly, at scale." Deel collects payments from employers and pays out those funds to contractors around the world. When an employe

For years, how employees get paid has been decided by what payroll infrastructure could handle - not by what people actually want. Employees have been asking for this. artifacts.normalized_text: # Shahriyar M. · LinkedIn · 2026-06-05 ## Author **Shahriyar M.** *Design @ Deel | Mentor | MSc in Computer Science* Current role: Staff Product Designer at Deel ## Post Yesterday we launched the new Deel app. We've been rebuilding it from the ground up with one goal in mind: reduce the time workers spend on the platform. Get what you need done, and move on with your day. The web and mobile are two different products now, and we're treating them that way. We've stopped chasing feature parity and started to ask what our users on mobile actually need. The main features in the app will now be designed with that principle in mind. I joined Deel in January and this has been the main project I've been working on. Six months to rebuild something this foundational, inside a company moving this fast, is not easy. I'm genuinely proud of the team. But, this is just the beginning. There is so much more coming soon. Including Akai as your AI partner. We're being deliberate with how we approach this on the app. More on that soon. P.S. I just requested time off through the app and it got approved.:) ## Top comments - Eric Sin: our company uses deel but i don't know much of the mobile app. what's the benefit of me using it over the webapp? - Eric Kabisch: Congrats Shahi! - Mariana R.: Amazing work, with an amazing, collaborative team <3 ## Mentions - Akai: (https://www.linkedin.com/company/akai-run) ## Engagement - Reactions: 108 - Comments: 12

When we started, the challenge was clear: recruiting at scale shouldn't be a grind. You shouldn't have to sift through thousands of applications to find the people who matter. What made this real was the collaboration. Our engineers moved fast and asked the right questions. Our designers obsessed over how recruiters actually work. Our talent acquisition team members told us exactly what was broken. Account executives kept us honest about what customers would actually use. And our Talent Ops folks gave us the ground truth every single day. 🩷 The 30% time savings is real, but what gets me is the shift: from tools that help you manage hiring faster to a system that thinks like your best recruiter. That's the difference between incremental improvement and rethinking what's possible. Thanks to everyone who made this happen. Together, we've built something customers will genuinely love! 💪 **Reshared from Alex Bouaziz:** > Last year Deel received over 1.3m applications and we're on track to receive 2m this year. artifacts.title: Deel Stablecoin Wallet Reduces Paycheck Erosion in Argentina | Nusabbeh Waqar posted on the topic | LinkedIn
artifacts.normalized_text: Deel Stablecoin Wallet Reduces Paycheck Erosion in Argentina | Nusabbeh Waqar posted on the topic | LinkedIn Agree & Join LinkedIn By clicking Continue to join or sign in, you agree to LinkedIn's User Agreement, Privacy Policy, and Cookie Policy. # Deel Stablecoin Wallet Reduces Paycheck Erosion in Argentina Senior Product Manager @ Deel | Building Scalable SaaS Products | Gen AI | Data & Reporting Workflows 3w - Report this post In Argentina, a paycheck starts losing value the moment it lands. Same job as someone in the US. By year end, 20 to 40% can just vanish. Not spent. Gone. And before a worker even uses that money, it can pass through ten different apps. Exchange it, move it, guard it against the rate. That is a tax on the people who can least afford it. Today Deel shipped something that goes right at this: a stablecoin wallet built into the app people already get paid in. Hold your pay in dollars, earn on it, and soon spend it anywhere with the Deel card. No crypto exchange, no extra accounts. It is not magic. You still convert back to local cash at some point. But it turns ten steps into one, and lets people hold dollars instead of watching their money shrink. Banks take 3 to 5 days to send money abroad. This takes seconds. Fewer middlemen between the work and the worker. Starting in Latin America, the rest of the world next. https://lnkd.in/e_Wqyz5m Redefining Pay: Hold, earn, and spend with Deel stablecoin deel.com To view or add a comment, sign in Sales & Operatio

Based in Netherlands [NL]. --- The number of entities we onboard onto Deel is on track to more than double by the end of this year. Our onboarding wasn't built for that. That's a hard job. My team runs Payroll Product Activation at Deel. We own the bridge from "deal signed" to "first payroll runs, on time and fully compliant." Right now that bridge is too slow and too manual. The bet my team is making: make going live feel almost instant, with AI doing the setup work instead of a queue of specialists. Ideally, we build the payroll before activation even starts. It's a hard, high-stakes problem (payroll is unforgiving), and it's growing faster than the old way of solving it can keep up. So I'm hiring a Senior Product Manager II who wants to take this challenge. I'm not handing someone a backlog to babysit. I want a builder who's shipped through real complexity, has the gut to experiment, and moves in weeks, not quarters. 5+ years in product, ideally fintech or infrastructure-heavy. Open to EMEA (or Asia where we can align hours). If this is the kind of problem you'd run at, DM me. If someone just came to mind, tag them. Link's in the comments to apply directly. ![Post by Menno Veen image](https://media.licdn.com/dms/ima

artifacts.normalized_text: Every company says they want to hire the best talent, anywhere in the world 🌎 Then someone asks: "Can we actually get them a visa?" Jira tickets. Spreadsheets. A 47-email thread with a law firm... | Mikita Manichau | 11 comments Agree & Join LinkedIn By clicking Continue to join or sign in, you agree to LinkedIn's User Agreement, Privacy Policy, and Cookie Policy. https://at.linkedin.com/in/mikitamanichau Mikita Manichau Director of Engineering @ Deel | I build products 0→1 with AI | I scale global distributed teams | I partner with businesses to drive technology transformation. 3mo Edited - Report this post Every company says they want to hire the best talent, anywhere in the world 🌎 Then someone asks: "Can we actually get them a visa?" Jira tickets. Spreadsheets. A 47-email thread with a law firm just to check eligibility. Three months later - no answer, candidate gone. That's not a hypothetical. That's most companies right now. I know this firsthand. When I relocated to Austria 🇦🇹 , my process was handled by a big incumbent provider. Email threads. Constantly pinging consultants. Zero visibility into where my case stood. No tracker, no timeline - just "we'll get back to you." That experience shaped how I think about what we're building at Deel. Last Thursday at The Big Deel 2026, we showed what 3 years of Deel Mobility became: self-serve managed immigration. In production. 100+ countries. Here's what we actually built 👇 🔍 Real-time eligibility checks - AI-powered screening

artifacts.title: Stephen Wünstel on LinkedIn
artifacts.normalized_text: # Stephen Wünstel · LinkedIn · 2026-06-25 ## Author **Stephen Wünstel** *Product Management @ Deel* Current role: Product Management at Deel ## Post Ship Whenever It's Ready: We shipped 15 small improvements without waiting for release gates. Running a 5-post series on Deel's AI stack-AKAI for operations automation + Claude for design and reasoning. How we ship faster, catch bugs upstream, and cut costs. Post 3/5. Approval gates treat everything the same: big strategic features and button label fixes get held at the same checkpoint. Both wait for the same meetings. Both need the same sign-off. We automated it. AKAI validates that small improvements and sometime even builds them-category fixes, dropdowns, error states-follow our patterns and won't break anything. If it passes, simplified approval. Is it good work? Ship it. We went from discovery, refinement and development to direct vibe coding and shipping whenever it's ready. Fifteen improvements that were sitting in a backlog waiting for "the right time"-better error messages, context-aware dropdowns, faster filtering. Individually none made the priorization but together they made a clear difference. What's the improvement sitting in your backlog that should've shipped three months ago? ## Engagement - Reactions: 7 - Comments: 0

artifacts.title: Saumil Sharma on LinkedIn
artifacts.normalized_text: # Saumil Sharma · LinkedIn · 2026-06-22 ## Author **Saumil Sharma** *Senior Product Manager | Deel* Current role: Sr. Product Manager at Deel ## Post Running payroll calculations with AI? Bold. I'd stay away though. Don't get me wrong...AI is amazing. I use it every day. But somewhere along the way, we started confusing "this is helpful" with "this can be trusted with money." Payroll is not a draft. It's not a recommendation. It's binary, you're either right or you're explaining to 500 employees why their salary looks experimental. AI is like a very smart intern: - great at triaging tickets - great at summarizing compliance updates - great at flagging "this looks off" - great at recommending what might be wrong But not the one you let: - calculate net pay - file taxes - approve final runs AI is probabilistic, payroll is not. One can be 95% right. The other gets escalations, penalties, and a very long Monday. Use AI to move faster. Just not in places where "close enough" becomes very expensive. ## Hashtags - #Payroll - #AI - #FutureOfWork ## Engagement - Reactions: 9 - Comments: 0

artifacts.title:
Product management at Deel is intense by design. Most weekends I'm still running at full speed when Friday ends. The only thing that fixes that is getting outside.

Long days, fast context switches… | Stephen A. Wünstel | 20 comments
artifacts.normalized_text: Product management at Deel is intense by design. Most weekends I'm still running at full speed when Friday ends. The only thing that fixes that is getting outside. Long days, fast context switches... | Stephen A. Wünstel | 20 comments Agree & Join LinkedIn By clicking Continue to join or sign in, you agree to LinkedIn's User Agreement, Privacy Policy, and Cookie Policy. # Stephen A. Wünstel's Post Product Management at Deel | AI | Human performance: mind, body, and remote teams | Berlin & Rio de Janeiro 4w Product management at Deel is intense by design. Most weekends I'm still running at full speed when Friday ends. The only thing that fixes that is getting outside. Long days, fast context switches, roadmap adjustments, quick action to solve customer problems and recently more and more AI and vibe coding. Every day is different. Every day is unexpected and exciting, but also intense. The kind of intensity that keeps you going and pushing until the weekend comes and you still can't slow down. For me, the answer has always been outside. I grew up in a little village surrounded by nature. That part never changed. What changed is that I now try to build my life around it on purpose. Working for remote companies over the last few years allowed me to travel with the good weather and spend more time outside. Like for many people, it started with the lockdown. I had just moved to Brazil to start a new job and suddenly everything was remote. During summer I went back to see my friend

artifacts.normalized_text: Y Combinator is the world's premier startup accelerator. The program has springboarded over 5,000 startups, valued at a combined $800 billion. But as it grew, the internal team got bogged down by effort-intensive administrative tasks, taking up valuable time needed to support YC's employees and founders. Y Combinator significantly reduced its admin workload with Rippling-their HR "tool of choice" The Problem The Solution Rippling minimized the administrative complexities of hiring, paying, and managing Y Combinator's workforce, enabling the startup accelerator to focus even more on its mission of supporting its founders. In this story Meet Y Combinator Launched in 2005, Y Combinator is Silicon Valley's preeminent "startup accelerator," helping founders all over the globe incubate startup ideas and grow innovative venture-backed businesses. The small cadre of admitted YC founders go through a rigorous three-month bootcamp laser-focused on building their product. In return, founders receive seed funding, mentorship, and a vast network of resources. The evidence of the program's success is clear. 45% of companies that come out of YC go on to raise Series A rounds of fundraising (compared to 33% of all VC-backed companies), and 4.5% of YC companies have gone on to become unicorns (compared to 2.5% elsewhere). artifacts.title: Chess.com creates frictionless people processes | Rippling
artifacts.normalized_text: In service of its mission, Chess.com aims to minimize friction for its team so they can focus on serving the global chess community. With key information about their workforce split across multiple places, the team recognized the need for scalable people processes to support their rapid growth. Chess.com creates frictionless people processes with a unified platform IndustryTechnology Number of Employees500-999 HeadquartersUnited States In this story - 01.Pain Points - 03.The Solution - 04.The Impact Pain Points - Making effective planning decisions due to headcount reporting limitations across team members - Tracking and managing a fully remote, global workforce, composed of contractors and full-time employees - Efficiently and securely provisioning technology without a dedicated IT team or resource The Challenge Between 2007 and 2019, Chess.com grew steadily to around 200 team members, with a mix of US-based employees and global contractors. The company was using separate, labor-intensive systems to oversee these two populations-an HRIS platform to manage employees and spreadsheets to manage contractors, who made up roughly 75% of the total team. With key information about its workforce split across multiple systems, Chess.com lacked unified visibility and a single source of truth for the full team. artifacts.title: Automate Role-Based Permissions and Approvals | Rippling
artifacts.normalized_text: We value your privacy. Learn more. PermissionsAutomate what employees can see and do. Securely. Permission profilesBuild permission profiles that give people the right power Define scope Scope is the people whose data a permission profile has some access to. It could be a reporting line or an entire department. Control access Access governs the different types of data a permission profile has about the people in its scope, usually tied to a specific app, like Payroll. Select actions Actions describe what a permission profile can do with the data in its scope-like view, edit, and approve-in the apps it has access to. SupergroupsA more secure, flexible way to permission Automatically assign permission profiles with Supergroups. Supergroups are dynamic membership lists built using attributes like level or location. As an employee's attributes change, their permissions update-sharing data securely and efficiently. Permission actionsEmpower people to do more than "view" Rippling grants access to data, not views. As a result, managers, HR business partners, and leaders can take action with data in their scope, building custom workflows, running their own reports, and more. Approval managementEscalate only the most important approvals Hire. Provision apps. Approve expenses. You set the rules for who can make and approve requests, under what circumstances, so small changes sail through while big-ticket items are flagged for review. artifacts.title: How a Scalable HR Solution Empowers a Dynamic Workforce at Barry's | Rippling
artifacts.normalized_text: Barry's, backed by Princeton Equity Group, has a fast-growing footprint and an equally dynamic employee population. Hourly workers often move between studios-or jump from studio staff to instructor roles-while leadership at HQ oversees everything from compliance to benefits to performance management. Barry's used a combination of tools for HCM, ATS, scheduling, time tracking, and benefits, creating cumbersome workflows. With over 8 new studios slated for 2025, Barry's needed a single, integrated platform that would grow alongside them. How a scalable HR solution empowers a dynamic workforce at Barry's The Problem The Solution With an entire HR tech stack-payroll, recruiting, time and attendance, scheduling, surveys, benefits administration, and more-unified within Rippling, Barry's has gained a simpler, more flexible approach to HR operations. In this story Meet Barry's At Barry's-famous for the high-intensity interval training workout that takes place in its "Red Room" across 15 countries-maintaining a premium fitness experience isn't just about the workout. It's also about supporting the employees behind the scenes: instructors, studio teams, and HQ employees. To manage a complex workforce at 53 US locations and counting, Barry's needed an HR and payroll platform that could adapt at speed, handle multi-entity requirements, and integrate a stack of tools all in one place. Senior Director of Employee Relations & Benefits Vanessa Sarne shares how Barry's chose Rippling to cent

Contact the press release distributor directly with any inquiries. # Introducing Rippling Travel: For Faster Bookings, Smarter Spending, and Better Travel People Center Inc. dba Rippling SAN FRANCISCO, July 31, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Rippling, a leading business software company, today announced the launch of Rippling Travel, allowing companies to save more time and money on business travel. Rippling Travel unifies expense management, corporate cards, bill pay, payroll, and travel booking all within a single, integrated system - making it faster, easier, and cheaper than ever to book and manage corporate travel. "At Rippling, our focus has always been to free people to work on hard problems by automating away repetitive admin work so they can focus on strategic work," said Matt Epstein, General Manager, Rippling Spend. That's why we launched Rippling Travel. It makes it insanely easy for employees to book flights, hotels, and cars without having to worry about what is (and isn't) in-policy. And for companies to automatically control every penny their employees spend on business travel, from in-flight seat upgrades to reimbursements on the road. Corporate travel has historically been a headache. For employees, it takes time and causes stress. For employers, it requires endless administrative tasks and back-and-forths on approvals. In

artifacts.title: Introducing Rippling Data Cloud | Rippling Product Launch - YouTube
artifacts.normalized_text: # Introducing Rippling Data Cloud | Rippling Product Launch - Channel: Rippling - Length: 01:19 - Views: 122 - Likes: 1 - Published: 2026-06-25T08:34:46-07:00 - Category: Science & Technology - Language: en ## Description Today we're launching Rippling Data Cloud, and with it, Rippling AI can now answer questions across any system you run your business on, not just the data that lives in Rippling. Pull in data from your CRM, POS, helpdesk system, or any other tool you use. You can answer questions like: 🤔 "Which engineering teams are spending the most on AI tools and have the least output to show for it?" ⏱️ "Which support managers have the fastest resolution times in customer tickets?" 📊 "Show me labor cost as a percent of sales across my stores and flag any stores that are understaffed." Data Cloud is the modern data stack, built into Rippling, with your org chart and permissions baked in and AI as the front door, so you can get the insight you need in minutes. artifacts.title: Rippling now wants to be your entire data stack | TechCrunch
artifacts.normalized_text: Rippling now wants to be your entire data stack | TechCrunch Image Credits:Bloomberg / Contributor / Getty Images Enterprise Copy Share Link # Rippling now wants to be your entire data stack Connie Loizos 9:00 AM PDT · June 25, 2026 Copy Share Link Parker Conrad wants you to believe that a huge chunk of data analytics belongs inside human capital management systems - a claim that conveniently positions Rippling, which started out as an HR software company, to compete directly with dedicated business intelligence tools. The pitch is that the modern data stack - the galaxy of tools that companies currently jury-rig from multiple vendors - can be collapsed into one. Just moving data from your various business systems into a warehouse is itself a massive industry; that's what companies like Fivetran and Airbyte do. Then you need somewhere to store and query it, like Snowflake; then something to transform and clean it, like dbt Labs; and then a visualization layer like Tableau on top. Conrad's argument is that Rippling knits together all of that into one system and wraps it in something the others lack: a built-in understanding of your org, its ever-evolving reporting structure, and everything impacted when any metric moves up or down. That's what Rippling Data Cloud, officially launching Thursday morning, is designed to deliver. To see it in action, Conrad shares his screen from his San Francisco office and then offers a window into what Rippling found when it turned the product

artifacts.title: Rippling Launches Data Cloud To Bring AI-Powered BI To Workforce Data
artifacts.normalized_text: Rippling Launches Data Cloud To Bring AI-Powered BI To Workforce Data # Rippling Launches Data Cloud To Bring AI-Powered BI To Workforce Data By Amit Chowdhry • Jun 26, 2026 Rippling Launches Data Cloud To Bring AI-Powered BI To Workforce Data Rippling announced the launch of Rippling Data Cloud, a new suite of products designed to bring AI-powered business intelligence to workforce and operational data. Rippling Data Cloud aggregates data from across a company into Rippling, connects it to worker identity, and makes it available for analysis, visualization, and action. The platform is designed to preserve and enrich business context so companies can answer nuanced questions about employees, teams, managers, departments, locations, roles, permissions, and historical organizational changes. Rippling said traditional BI infrastructure often struggles with questions that depend on understanding who a piece of business data is connected to and what the organization looked like at the time an event occurred. For example, sales performance, support resolution times, engineering velocity, overtime trends, customer complaints, and other business metrics often require employee and organizational context to be interpreted correctly. The company said this challenge has become more important as AI becomes more central to analytics. Without governed business context, AI systems may struggle to answer questions accurately across operational systems. Rippling Data Cloud includes data connec

artifacts.title: Post by Matt MacInnis
artifacts.normalized_text: # Post by Matt MacInnis · LinkedIn · 2026-06-22 **Matt MacInnis**: COO at Rippling with 24 years 4 months of experience. Previous roles include Founder And CEO at Inkling Systems, Sr. Manager, International Education Markets at Apple, and Sr. Manager, Asia Education Marketing at Apple. Based in San Francisco, California, United States [US]. --- Today we launched Business Banking*, which is going to simplify the lives of finance, payroll, and HR teams (and their AI agents!). I want to explain why. From the outside, payroll has always seemed simpler than it is. In reality, it's full of hidden complexity for every company. One example is that payroll systems make you process each pay run two or more days in advance, which means you're constantly trying to jam things in before the cutoff. Spot bonuses are out of luck if they come in even a minute late, and need to be pushed to the next run. If you catch an error after the window closes, an employee has to wait extra days for their paycheck. Business Banking fixes that. By connecting your operating cash directly to [Rippling](https://linkedin.com/company/rippling) Payroll, the day you run payroll is the day your employees get paid. Payroll admins can make changes right up until payday. But same-day payroll is only half the story. Most business bank accounts pay you less than 0.07% on your operating cash**. With Business Banking, you automatically earn 2.25% APY***, 32x the national average**, your deposits are covered by up to $20

artifacts.normalized_text: # Suraj Savalia · LinkedIn · 2026-07-01 ## Author **Suraj Savalia** *Data + AI, Product at Rippling* Current role: Product Lead at Rippling ## Post Last week we launched Rippling Data Cloud -- an all-in-one data and AI platform built for operators. It's not just for HR teams; it's truly for everyone. I'm incredibly proud of the team I've had the privilege of working with to build this, and excited by the early value customers are already getting. I was recently looking back at a strategy doc I wrote 3.5 years ago, and I was surprised by how consistent the core vision has remained. The language has evolved, but the principles haven't. At Rippling, we've always held two beliefs that drive our product decisions: 1. Companies are idiosyncratic. Customers need software that can adapt to the way they work. 2. We should build great, opinionated defaults so customers don't have to configure everything from scratch. Applied to data and AI, that means a platform that lets customers quickly connect third-party data and immediately start asking AI questions about it. We handle the hard work of building the context behind the scenes so customers don't have to. At the same time, customers still have the flexibility to write SQL against any of their data or use AI to build flexible dashboards. Flexible when you need it. Opinionated when you don't. artifacts.title: Lessons from deploying AI at Rippling: start narrow, iterate, reuse ...
artifacts.normalized_text: In the last eight months, we have taken multiple AI use-cases from idea to production at Rippling, not demos, not prototypes, but real systems used daily by real teams. | Ankur Bhatt Agree & Join LinkedIn By clicking Continue to join or sign in, you agree to LinkedIn's User Agreement, Privacy Policy, and Cookie Policy. # Lessons from deploying AI at Rippling: start narrow, iterate, reuse, review, own, and learn AI Engineering Leader | Applied AI & Platform Architecture | CTO Experience 6mo In the last eight months, we have taken multiple AI use-cases from idea to production at Rippling, not demos, not prototypes, but real systems used daily by real teams. If I take a step back... the same patterns show up every single time. Here are the lessons that matter. ⸻ 1. Start with one sharp problem AI only works when the scope is painfully narrow. One constraint and one measurable success metric. Every strong system begins there. 2. Iteration beats novelty The biggest improvements didn't come from new models. They came from disciplined iteration: better prompts, tighter thresholds, cleaner data paths. Craft over hype, every time. 👉 In one system, a single tightening of a threshold removed a failure mode that had persisted for weeks. Small changes, big leverage. 3. Reuse the foundation Teams that reused existing pipelines, orchestration, and monitoring moved 10x faster, while teams that rebuilt everything slowed themselves before v1 even shipped. 4. Treat human review as a feature The

artifacts.title: rippling
artifacts.normalized_text: Thuis • Rippling ## It takes tribe ; #### wij zijn wevers #### we bestaan bij de gratie van relatie #### de Grote Omwenteling vraagt ons alles te geven #### hoe maak jij je leven weer verweven? De Gedragen Stam is een hele mooie plek om te oefenen in het samenleven met anderen. Voor mij is het een positief-turbulente reis. Ik heb een stap kunnen maken in mijn eigen man-zijn. Ik heb veel vertrouwen dat iedereen die daarvoor open staat, geconfronteerd zal worden met zichzelf op een liefdevolle manier. Rene Nierop Zo ongelooflijk waardevol, koesterend en liefdevol. Ik voel ook dat het belangrijk is voor de mensheid om weer te leren op deze manier samen te zijn. Herman Wijffels Wauw, het heeft me zoveel gebracht. Ik heb tijdens de Gedragen Stam twee keer een vrij radicale shift meegemaakt. Mede daardoor is de groep niet meer te intens of te veel. Ik voel ruimte in mezelf en kan nu vanuit mijn eigen gronding aanwezig zijn. Melissa van der Beek Zooo professioneel en kundig maar wel in krachtige en doorleefde eenvoud. Het was ronduit prachtig. Het voelt een beetje alsof ik weer iets terug heb, wat ik kwijtgeraakt was, als een soul retrieval. Nanke Lavrijsen ##### Rippling is een levendige gemeenschap mensen die een cultuur van gezamenlijke gedragenheid praktiseren als fundament voor een florerende samenleving. ## Agenda Tribe avonden #### Ervaar de magie van gezamenlijke gedragenheid Dans elkaar thuis #### » Cirkelhart | Do 9 juli | 17.00u-21.30u | Maarn» Dans elkaar thuis | Do 10 s

Altération tente de figer l'oeuvre du temps et de capturer les transformations de la matière. Ce qui nous lie Deux lianes s'entremêlent, la promesse d'un amour indéfectible Des brins d'herbe s'enchevêtrent comme la danse de nos cheveux dans le vent Ta main dans la mienne. Nous sommes vivants. Créatures Une corolle déployée comme une spirale qui cacherait en son coeur un secret, un coquillage peut-être, trouvé là... ou alors des herbes aquatiques agitées par les courants... theme_comparison.note: shares = count / n classified artifacts of that company; cross-company bars compare shares, raw counts shown alongside

temporal_baseline.note: Prior membership = real archive-capture/published date inside the comparison window; undated live content is current. Theme counts = artifacts carrying the theme as primary OR supporting (the same rule the change events use). Windows have different sample sizes (14 vs 108) — compare shares, not counts; treat emergence/recession as signals. 1 dated artifact(s) fall outside both windows and are excluded.

proof_gaps.actionable_interpretation: Deel repeats “workforce_visibility” with weak observed proof and Rippling's proof is none. Treat as a proof-building play — the public evidence does not yet establish who can prove it better.
proof_gaps.why_attack_might_backfire: If Deel holds unpublished proof, a comparative claim invites a documented counterattack; comparative copy also requires legal substantiation.

Don't attack the claim head-on — reframe around a structural advantage, or concede this ground. (Strong competitor proof here is what the evidence shows; it is not a claim about Rippling's underlying capability.)

opportunities.title: The Custom Workflow Builder Demo: Show a Live No-Code App Solving a Global Hiring Edge Case Deel's 7 Products Can't Configure
opportunities.competitor_pattern: Deel repeats “Deel has launched 7 new integrated products to solve the complexity of global hiring, payroll, compliance, and workforce management.” with weak observed public proof.
opportunities.change_over_time: see change events; message–proof gap is current-state unless a change event is linked

staged_plan.objective: Produce a demo-grade workflow page and video showing a real (or realistic composite) customer using Rippling's no-code app builder to solve a specific global hiring/payroll exception (e.g., country-specific approval routing, compliance escalation).
proceed_if: A customer or sales-engineering example exists that can be shown end-to-end without fabrication
Legal/comparative-claims review confirms no direct superiority language is used
The demo clearly maps to a job Deel's 7-product announcement claims to solve
stop_or_reframe_if: No real customer or credible composite workflow can be found to demo
Legal flags the framing as an implied superiority claim that can't be substantiated

staged_plan.objective: Test the demo/page with a small set of target buyers (analysts, existing customers in eval cycles, sales calls) to see if the configurability angle resonates and holds up against Deel comparison questions.
proceed_if: Buyers in live sales cycles reference the demo positively as a differentiator
No material factual pushback from prospects who have evaluated Deel's new products directly
stop_or_reframe_if: Prospects report Deel has comparable configurability not reflected in our evidence
Sales reports the angle causes confusion rather than clarity about Rippling's value

staged_plan.objective: Publish the workflow/demo page broadly and equip sales with the asset for competitive deals against Deel in active evaluations.
proceed_if: Validate_proof stage shows positive buyer reaction and no factual contradiction
Sales engineering confirms the demo is repeatable across multiple prospect conversations
stop_or_reframe_if: Deel publicly launches a comparable no-code/configurability capability that undermines the differentiation
Win-rate or deal-cycle data shows no measurable impact after a full sales cycle of use

product_comparability.competitor_product: Deel's 7 newly launched integrated products (global hiring, payroll, compliance, workforce management)
product_comparability.focal_product: Rippling custom internal app builder (no-code) applied to global HR/payroll workflows
product_comparability.shared_buyer_job: Solve global hiring, payroll, and compliance complexity within a single platform
overlapping_capabilities: Native product breadth across HR/payroll/compliance/workforce management
materially_different_capabilities: Rippling's no-code custom app/workflow builder has strong observed proof; no equivalent no-code configurability capability is evidenced in Deel's supplied proof (which is feature assertions, one customer story, one demo, and one unsupported assertion)
product_comparability.rationale: Deel's claim is about the number and breadth of native products; Rippling's strongest proof is about configurability/extensibility within its platform. These are not the same axis of comparison — one is about product count, the other about workflow flexibility — so a direct head-to-head equivalence claim would overreach. The action should frame this as a different, complementary axis of value (configurability) rather than claim Rippling's breadth is superior to Deel's 7 products.

opportunities.category_entry_point: "When our global hiring/payroll process has an exception the vendor's standard product doesn't handle"
target_personas: Head of People Ops / HRIS Owner
VP HR / CHRO at multi-country employer
target_jobs: Automate a workflow-specific HR/payroll exception without engineering help
Evaluate platform flexibility before consolidating HR systems
Reduce reliance on IT/dev tickets for HR process changes
focal_product_focus: Rippling App Shop / Custom Apps (no-code workflow builder)
opportunities.message_angle: Deel is marketing breadth — 7 new integrated products announced to cover global hiring, payroll, compliance, and workforce management. But announcing more products is not the same as giving customers the ability to shape any one of them to their actual workflow. Rippling's proof point on this theme is a strong, demonstrable capability: build custom internal apps without code to automate workflow-specific processes. The action is to make that capability tangible and comparable — show, in a live demo, a specific global HR/payroll edge case (e.g., a multi-country approval exception or compliance escalation) being solved via a no-code custom app, and let the buyer judge configurability vs. product-count.
opportunities.villain_or_status_quo: The status quo narrative in the category is "more native products = more solved complexity." Buyers are being sold product count as a proxy for capability, without evidence that any single product flexes to their specific process.
opportunities.promised_transformation: From: evaluating vendors by counting how many native modules they've shipped → To: evaluating vendors by whether the platform can be configured, without engineering resources, to fit the buyer's actual workflow exceptions.
opportunities.why_competitor_cannot_easily_copy: Deel's current public proof on this theme is concentrated in feature assertions and a single customer story/demo — there is no evidence in the supplied material of an equivalent no-code custom-app framework. Building genuine no-code extensibility across newly-launched, still-maturing products would require platform-level investment, not a marketing update, and Rippling's proof here is already described as strong, giving a real (if not unlimited) head start.
opportunities.why_this_could_backfire: Deel could respond by showcasing its own configurability features (if any exist but weren't in this evidence set), reframing the comparison as apples-to-apples and neutralizing the differentiation. Additionally, because comparability is weak (product breadth vs. configurability are different value axes), a buyer or analyst could call out the comparison as a deflection rather than a direct rebuttal to Deel's breadth claim — Rippling must be careful to position this as "a different lens on complexity" rather than implying its custom app builder makes Deel's 7 products redundant.
opportunities.experiment_hypothesis: If Rippling publishes a concrete, demoable proof of its no-code custom app builder solving a global hiring/payroll workflow exception, then prospects evaluating Deel's newly launched 7 products will perceive Rippling as offering a more configurable (not just broader) platform, increasing consideration-stage engagement and sales-assisted win rate in competitive deals against Deel.
opportunities.primary_metric: Sales-assisted win rate in active Deel-competitive opportunities where the demo/asset was used
guardrail_metrics: Deal cycle length in Deel-competitive opportunities
Prospect-reported confusion or pushback flagged by sales
Legal/compliance flags on comparative claims post-launch
opportunities.minimum_sample_rule: At least 15 sales-assisted opportunities where the demo/asset was used against Deel before evaluating win-rate impact
opportunities.scale_rule: If win rate in Deel-competitive deals improves by a meaningful margin (e.g., double-digit percentage point lift) with no legal/compliance issues after the minimum sample, expand distribution to broader campaign channels and analyst briefings
opportunities.iterate_rule: If buyer feedback indicates the workflow example chosen doesn't map to their most pressing pain point, iterate the demo with alternate workflow scenarios (e.g., compliance escalation vs. approval routing) before re-testing
opportunities.kill_rule: If validate_proof stage surfaces credible evidence that Deel has an equivalent no-code configurability capability, or if legal review cannot clear the asset without diluting it into a non-differentiated statement, kill the campaign-launch stage and revert to internal enablement only

opportunities.title: "Certified, Not Just Claimed": IT Security Demand-Gen Push Targeting Deel Endpoint-Security Claims
opportunities.competitor_pattern: Deel repeats “Deel IT automatically applies comprehensive endpoint protection across your device fleet with minimal manual effort, maintaining consistent security policies from device shipment through retirement.” with weak observed public proof.
opportunities.change_over_time: see change events; message–proof gap is current-state unless a change event is linked

audience: IT and security decision-makers at mid-market companies (200-2000 employees) currently evaluating or renewing global HR/IT/EOR platforms
Security-conscious buyers in industries with compliance sensitivity (fintech, healthcare-adjacent, regulated services)
campaign_plan.category_entry_point: When evaluating global HR/IT platforms for device security compliance during vendor security review or audit season
message: Don't just take a vendor's word on endpoint security — ask what's actually certified and audited.
Rippling backs its device security claims with industry certifications and regular independent audits.
Evaluating a global HR/IT platform? Ask for the certification, not just the claim.
channels: LinkedIn ABM ads targeting IT/security titles
Search ads on security-certification and vendor-comparison keywords
Retargeting ads to visitors of security/compliance content
Email nurture sequence to IT segment in existing CRM pipeline
campaign_plan.creative_or_asset: Short-form ad set (LinkedIn + display) featuring a 'claim vs. certification' visual checklist prompting buyers to ask vendors for specific certification names and audit dates; links to a landing page, not a head-to-head comparison page.
campaign_plan.landing_experience: Landing page titled 'What to Ask Before You Trust a Vendor's Security Claims' — features Rippling's specific certifications and audit cadence, a short buyer checklist of questions to ask any HR/IT vendor about device security, and a CTA to talk to a security specialist. Avoids direct Deel naming or comparison table.
campaign_plan.primary_metric: Marketing-qualified leads (MQLs) from IT/security persona segment attributable to campaign

staged_plan.objective: Confirm and document Rippling's specific certifications, audit cadence, and device-lifecycle security policy evidence in a form usable in ad claims and landing page copy.
proceed_if: Rippling has named, verifiable certifications (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001) with current audit dates available for public reference
Legal/compliance confirms specific claims are substantiated and safe for comparative-adjacent framing
stop_or_reframe_if: Certifications are outdated, in-progress, or cannot be publicly cited
Legal flags that current proof cannot support even non-comparative security claims

staged_plan.objective: Test message resonance and claim clarity with a small paid pilot before scaling spend.
proceed_if: CTR and landing page engagement on security-certification messaging meet or exceed account benchmark for IT/security campaigns
No legal or customer pushback on claim framing during pilot window
stop_or_reframe_if: Engagement is materially below benchmark, indicating the angle doesn't resonate
Prospects or reviewers challenge the accuracy or fairness of the comparative framing

staged_plan.objective: Scale the validated campaign across full channel mix to IT/security personas in mid-market segment.
proceed_if: Pilot cost-per-qualified-lead is at or below existing paid demand-gen benchmarks
Sales reports leads are qualified and referencing the security/certification message positively
stop_or_reframe_if: Cost-per-qualified-lead exceeds benchmark by a significant margin after full ramp
Sales feedback indicates leads are low-intent or message causes confusion about product scope

product_comparability.competitor_product: Deel IT (endpoint protection / device fleet management)
product_comparability.shared_buyer_job: Ensure company-issued devices remain secure and compliant from shipment through retirement
product_comparability.rationale: Both vendors claim endpoint security and lifecycle policy enforcement, but the evidence base differs sharply: Deel's claim is supported mostly by feature assertions with limited independent verification, while Rippling's claim is supported by named certifications and audit processes. This is a proof-strength gap, not a demonstrated capability gap — Rippling should not claim Deel lacks the capability, only that its own claim is more substantiated on this specific theme.

opportunities.category_entry_point: "When evaluating global HR/IT platforms for device security compliance during vendor security review or audit season"
target_personas: VP People/HR Ops evaluating IT+HR consolidation
target_jobs: Vet device/endpoint security posture during HRIS/IT platform evaluation
Confirm compliance and audit readiness before consolidating IT + workforce systems
Reduce vendor risk exposure tied to distributed/remote device fleets
channels: Email nurture to IT security segment in existing pipeline
opportunities.message_angle: Deel IT's endpoint protection claims are backed mostly by feature assertions and a single customer story, not third-party certification. Rippling can lead with its documented industry certifications and regular audits as the evidence-backed reason security-conscious IT buyers should scrutinize vendor security claims before trusting device-lifecycle security to a payroll-first HR platform.
opportunities.villain_or_status_quo: IT and security leaders take vendor security marketing claims at face value during vendor selection, without asking for the certification and audit evidence behind "automatic" endpoint protection promises.
opportunities.promised_transformation: From: evaluating device-security claims based on vendor marketing assertions → To: evaluating device-security claims based on demonstrable certifications and audit records.
opportunities.why_competitor_cannot_easily_copy: Certifications and audit records require sustained compliance investment and cannot be marketed credibly overnight; Deel would need to complete and disclose equivalent third-party audits/certifications to close this proof gap, which is a longer-cycle structural commitment, not a messaging change.
opportunities.why_this_could_backfire: Deel may already hold relevant certifications not captured in the supplied evidence (the gap reflects observed proof distribution, not an audited absence of certification) — if Deel publishes or already has equivalent certification claims, Rippling's implicit "more substantiated" framing could look either redundant or, worse, factually wrong, inviting a direct rebuttal. The campaign must avoid stating or implying Deel is uncertified.
opportunities.experiment_hypothesis: If Rippling promotes its certified, audited security posture as a buyer-education angle (rather than a direct competitive claim), IT/security personas evaluating global HR/IT platforms will engage at a higher rate and convert to qualified pipeline more efficiently than generic security messaging, because the underweighted proof point is genuinely differentiated on evidence strength.
opportunities.primary_metric: Marketing-qualified leads (MQLs) from IT/security persona segment
guardrail_metrics: Cost per qualified lead vs. existing demand-gen benchmark
opportunities.minimum_sample_rule: Pilot must reach at least 5,000 ad impressions and 50 landing page sessions per channel before evaluating engagement metrics for statistical reliability.
opportunities.scale_rule: Scale spend and expand to additional channels only if pilot cost-per-qualified-lead is at or below existing IT/security demand-gen benchmark and sales confirms lead quality over a 2-week minimum follow-up window.
opportunities.iterate_rule: If engagement is moderate but conversion to qualified lead is weak, revise landing page checklist and CTA copy, and retest with the same audience before altering channel mix.
opportunities.kill_rule: Kill or fully reframe the campaign if legal/compliance identifies claim risk during the pilot, if cost-per-qualified-lead exceeds benchmark by more than 50% after two optimization cycles, or if Deel publicly demonstrates equivalent/superior certification evidence that undermines the campaign's premise.

opportunities.title: Deel Goals/Engage Objection-Handling Battlecard + Discovery Guide for Workforce Visibility Deals
opportunities.competitor_pattern: Deel repeats “Goals in Engage provides built-in goal-setting and tracking across multiple organizational levels to create alignment, clarity, and measurable progress without manual effort.” with weak observed public proof.
opportunities.change_over_time: see change events; message–proof gap is current-state unless a change event is linked

staged_plan.objective: Interview 5-8 AEs/SEs who have faced the Deel Goals/Engage objection in live deals; compile actual prospect questions and Deel's stated capability language into a discovery-question battlecard draft, and confirm with Rippling product team what (if anything) exists today for goal-tracking/alignment features.
proceed_if: At least 3 independent deal instances confirm the objection is recurring and costing momentum in deals
Product team confirms current state (missing/partial/available) of any comparable Rippling capability so reps are not left exposed
stop_or_reframe_if: Fewer than 2 reps report ever encountering this objection in the last quarter, suggesting low deal relevance
Product team reveals a roadmap commitment that would make a full feature-comparison asset more appropriate than a discovery-only battlecard

staged_plan.objective: Pilot the battlecard/discovery guide with a small group of AEs (5-10) on live deals where the Deel goals objection surfaces; track whether the discovery questions help reps advance or neutralize the objection without making unsupported claims.
proceed_if: Pilot reps report the discovery questions successfully shift the conversation away from feature-parity claims toward the prospect's actual requirements in at least half of tracked deals
No legal/compliance flags raised on the language used
stop_or_reframe_if: Reps report prospects push back hard when Rippling cannot answer 'what do you offer instead,' damaging credibility
Legal flags any claim in the asset as unsupported or risky

staged_plan.objective: Roll out the finalized battlecard and discovery guide org-wide via enablement platform and SKO training, with a feedback loop for reps to flag new Deel claims or proof points as they emerge.
proceed_if: Validation stage shows measurable improvement in deal progression rate or objection-handling confidence scores
Enablement leadership signs off on broad distribution
stop_or_reframe_if: Deel materially strengthens its proof (e.g., adds multiple named case studies or third-party validation) such that the battlecard's premise of 'weak proof' becomes outdated
Sales feedback indicates the asset is rarely used or not moving deals

product_comparability.shared_buyer_job: Set and track goals/alignment across organizational levels for a distributed workforce
overlapping_capabilities: Goal-setting workflows referenced in Deel's marketing claim
product_comparability.rationale: Deel's claim is specifically about a named 'Goals in Engage' feature with its own (weak) proof distribution. Rippling has no observed proof point or confirmed shipped capability on this exact theme -- only a job posting signal for hourly-worker workforce management design leads, which is not evidence of a goal-tracking feature. Treating this as apples-to-apples would overstate Rippling's position; the action must be framed as competitive discovery/objection-handling, not feature-for-feature comparison.

opportunities.category_entry_point: Prospect mentions Deel's Goals/Engage feature or asks how Rippling handles goal-setting and cross-org alignment during an active deal cycle
target_jobs: Win competitive deals against Deel where goals/alignment is raised
Qualify whether a prospect's org-structure needs are actually served by Deel's Goals/Engage feature
Avoid unsubstantiated competitive claims that create legal or credibility risk
focal_product_focus: Rippling core HRIS / workforce management (goal-tracking capability status to be confirmed with product)
opportunities.message_angle: When Deel reps lead with "Goals in Engage gives you built-in goal-setting and alignment tracking across the org, without manual effort," Rippling's field team needs a structured way to probe the actual evidence behind that claim (which today is thin: two feature assertions, one customer story, one quantified outcome, one unsupported assertion) rather than concede the ground or overclaim Rippling's own unified position. The angle is not "Rippling is better at goals tracking" — Rippling has no comparable proof point on this theme yet — but rather "let's test whether Deel's alignment claim holds up under the customer's actual multi-level, multi-country reporting workflow," using discovery questions that surface gaps distributors of HR software commonly find in point/expansion modules bolted onto payroll-and-EOR cores.
opportunities.villain_or_status_quo: Sales reps currently have no structured response when prospects raise Deel's Goals/Engage alignment pitch; the default is either silence or an unsupported counter-claim about Rippling being "more unified," which risks credibility if challenged.
opportunities.promised_transformation: From: Rippling AEs improvising or overclaiming when Deel's goals-alignment pitch comes up in-deal → To: Rippling AEs running a consistent, evidence-based discovery sequence that tests the depth of Deel's goal-tracking claim against the prospect's real org-structure and reporting needs, without Rippling asserting capabilities or superiority it hasn't proven.
opportunities.why_competitor_cannot_easily_copy: This is not about a moat Deel can't copy -- it's an internal enablement asset. The defensibility comes from Rippling's sales org building a repeatable discovery methodology (questions that expose whether Deel's alignment claim generalizes to complex org structures) faster and more rigorously than ad hoc rep talking points, which is an execution advantage, not a structural one.
opportunities.why_this_could_backfire: If reps use the battlecard to imply Rippling has superior or equivalent goal-tracking capability without any shipped proof, prospects or Deel could call out the bluff, and it could surface publicly as an unsubstantiated claim, damaging trust. There is also risk that probing Deel's "weak proof" comes across as deflection if Rippling cannot show its own roadmap or capability in this area when directly asked.
opportunities.experiment_hypothesis: If Rippling AEs are equipped with a structured discovery-question battlecard targeting the specific weak points in Deel's Goals/Engage proof (feature assertions without depth, one customer story, one quantified outcome, one unsupported assertion), then reps will more effectively neutralize the objection in live deals without resorting to unsupported competitive claims, improving deal confidence and progression on workforce-visibility-themed opportunities.
opportunities.primary_metric: Rep-reported objection-handling success rate (deal advanced / objection neutralized) on deals where Deel Goals/Engage is raised
guardrail_metrics: Number of legal/compliance flags on battlecard language
Deal cycle length for affected opportunities (should not increase)
opportunities.minimum_sample_rule: At least 15 tracked deal instances where the Deel Goals/Engage objection is raised and the battlecard is used, before evaluating success rate
opportunities.scale_rule: If pilot shows >=50% improvement in objection neutralization rate and zero unresolved legal flags across the minimum sample, roll out to full sales org via SKO and CRM embed
opportunities.iterate_rule: If success rate is between 25-50% or reps request different framing/questions, revise discovery questions and re-pilot with a fresh set of deals for one more cycle before deciding to scale or kill
opportunities.kill_rule: If fewer than 25% of piloted deals show improved objection handling, or if legal/compliance identifies unresolvable risk in the claims framing, retire the asset and default reps to a neutral 'we don't have a comparable proof point yet' response

limitations: Run ended budget-bounded (hit the research-time cap) after 19 iterations covering 9 source classes; 3 dimensions remain unattempted: historical_pricing, historical_product, out_of_home
negative_observations: Similarweb-via-Exa returned a payload for 'deel.com' but no requested metric fields were present; nothing synthesized. (superseded: a later Similarweb call succeeded — see Traffic & channel mix)

meta_ads: live collection is not configured (US commercial active ads are exposed only through the public Meta Ad Library UI; the API covers worldwide political/social-issue ads and UK/EU commercial windows only — no general commercial-ad API exists (§39.7). Use a recorded fixture or compliant UI capture.); no creatives were fabricated. Provide a recorded fixture or compliant capture for coverage.
4 google_ads discovery artifacts excluded at render (FAQ/blank/other-advertiser transparency pages); retained /advertiser pages are unverified discovery pointers
